summary: Fencing improves accountability, but can become costly bureaucracy or false assurance if its record and checks are incomplete.

points.text: Logging every event can expose sensitive inputs or create retention obligations. Apply data minimization, access controls and retention limits to the history itself.
A trust score is a decision aid, not a safety proof. It must be recalibrated from outcomes and must never silently widen authority after a model or provider changes.
Too many approval gates slow safe work and encourage bypasses. Keep escalation targeted to irreversible effects, sensitive data and missing or conflicting evidence.
